name: WebEyeTrack
tagline: Lightweight CNN-based gaze estimation with few-shot on-device personalization
short_impression: WebEyeTrack is an open-source framework that uses a lightweight CNN-based neural network to predict gaze points on screen. It offers both Python and JavaScript/TypeScript implementations, enabling real-time eye tracking in web browsers with few-shot gaze estimation capabilities. The framework supports on-device personalization and unseen person adaptation, making it particularly suitable for HCI applications and research.
pros: Few-shot learning with minimal calibration samples
Dual implementation (Python for research, JavaScript for deployment)
Lightweight CNN architecture suitable for real-time inference
Supports both desktop and browser-based applications
cons: Smaller community compared to established tools (19 GitHub stars)
Newer tool with less extensive validation
privacy_notes: On-device processing with personalization. All computation occurs locally without requiring external servers.
